119	    // Verify spilled x28 (expected = 0xA000_..._28).
120	    bnez    x29, check_x29
121	    ld      x28, 8(x5)
122	    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000
123	    addi    x6, x6, 28
124	    beq     x28, x6, check_x29
125	    addi    x29, x0, 28
126

128	    // Verify spilled x29 (expected = 0xA000_..._29).
129	    // Note: x29 is the result reg; if non-zero, skip but keep prior fail.
130	    bnez    x29, exit_build
131	    ld      x28, 16(x5)
132	    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000
133	    addi    x6, x6, 29

file.content:
// rt_all_gprs.S
//
// Tier-1 fixture: full sweep of x1..x31 across QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2.
// Each register survives the round-trip with its 64-bit value intact.
//
// Phase A (QEMU1):
//   x5 = 0x80002000 (also serves as Phase C spill base).
//   x_i = 0xA000_0000_0000_0000 + i for i in {1..4, 6..31}.
// Phase B (vsim):  no-op.
// Phase C (QEMU2):
//   Spill x6, x28, x29 (sacrificed below).
//   Verify x_i directly for i in {1..4, 7..27, 30..31, then 5}.
//   Verify spilled x6, x28, x29 against expected.
//   Exit code = first mismatching reg num, or 0 on full pass.

#include "semihost_exit.inc"

.section .text
.globl _start
_start:
    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000   // temp base for ADDI sweep
    addi    x1,  x6, 1
    addi    x2,  x6, 2
    addi    x3,  x6, 3
    addi    x4,  x6, 4
    addi    x7,  x6, 7
    addi    x8,  x6, 8
    addi    x9,  x6, 9
    addi    x10, x6, 10
    addi    x11, x6, 11
    addi    x12, x6, 12
    addi    x13, x6, 13
    addi    x14, x6, 14
    addi    x15, x6, 15
    addi    x16, x6, 16
    addi    x17, x6, 17
    addi    x18, x6, 18
    addi    x19, x6, 19
    addi    x20, x6, 20
    addi    x21, x6, 21
    addi    x22, x6, 22
    addi    x23, x6, 23
    addi    x24, x6, 24
    addi    x25, x6, 25
    addi    x26, x6, 26
    addi    x27, x6, 27
    addi    x28, x6, 28
    addi    x29, x6, 29
    addi    x30, x6, 30
    addi    x31, x6, 31
    addi    x6,  x6, 6               // x6 = 0xA000_..._6
    li      x5, 0x80002000           // x5 = spill base (also its expected value)

    csrwi   0x7C0, 0                 // QEMU1 drains, exits

    csrwi   0x7C0, 1                 // vsim drains, exits 200

phase_c:
    // Spill x6, x28, x29 to 0x80002000, +8, +16 before corrupting them.
    sd      x6,  0(x5)
    sd      x28, 8(x5)
    sd      x29, 16(x5)

    li      x6,  0xA000000000000000  // verification base
    li      x29, 0                   // result reg (0 = pass)

    .macro CHECK_BASE n
        bnez    x29, 99f
        addi    x28, x6, \n
        beq     x\n, x28, 99f
        addi    x29, x0, \n
    99:
    .endm

    CHECK_BASE 1
    CHECK_BASE 2
    CHECK_BASE 3
    CHECK_BASE 4
    CHECK_BASE 7
    CHECK_BASE 8
    CHECK_BASE 9
    CHECK_BASE 10
    CHECK_BASE 11
    CHECK_BASE 12
    CHECK_BASE 13
    CHECK_BASE 14
    CHECK_BASE 15
    CHECK_BASE 16
    CHECK_BASE 17
    CHECK_BASE 18
    CHECK_BASE 19
    CHECK_BASE 20
    CHECK_BASE 21
    CHECK_BASE 22
    CHECK_BASE 23
    CHECK_BASE 24
    CHECK_BASE 25
    CHECK_BASE 26
    CHECK_BASE 27
    CHECK_BASE 30
    CHECK_BASE 31

    // Verify x5 directly (still holds 0x80002000).
    bnez    x29, check_spilled
    li      x28, 0x80002000
    beq     x5, x28, check_spilled
    addi    x29, x0, 5

check_spilled:
    // Verify spilled x6 (expected = 0xA000_..._6).
    bnez    x29, check_x28
    ld      x28, 0(x5)
    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000
    addi    x6, x6, 6
    beq     x28, x6, check_x28
    addi    x29, x0, 6

check_x28:
    // Verify spilled x28 (expected = 0xA000_..._28).
    bnez    x29, check_x29
    ld      x28, 8(x5)
    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000
    addi    x6, x6, 28
    beq     x28, x6, check_x29
    addi    x29, x0, 28

check_x29:
    // Verify spilled x29 (expected = 0xA000_..._29).
    // Note: x29 is the result reg; if non-zero, skip but keep prior fail.
    bnez    x29, exit_build
    ld      x28, 16(x5)
    li      x6, 0xA000000000000000
    addi    x6, x6, 29
    beq     x28, x6, exit_build
    addi    x29, x0, 29

exit_build:
    mv      a2, x29
    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2

content.content:
1	// Shared exit-via-semihosting macro for hybrid round-trip fixtures.
2	//
3	// SEMIHOST_EXIT exit_reg
4	//   Builds the {reason=ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit, exit_code=<exit_reg>}
5	//   16-byte block at 0x80001000, sets a0=SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED (0x20), a1=block,
6	//   then issues the RISC-V semihosting `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`
7	//   triple. exit_reg may be any GPR holding the desired host exit code; this
8	//   macro clobbers t0, t1, a0, a1.
9	//
10	// QEMU's host-exit returns the low 8 bits of <exit_reg> as the process status.
11	
12	.macro SEMIHOST_EXIT exit_reg
13	    li      t0, 0x80001000
14	    li      t1, 0x20026                     // ADP_Stopped_ApplicationExit
15	    sd      t1, 0(t0)
16	    sd      \exit_reg, 8(t0)
17	    li      a0, 0x20                        // SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED
18	    mv      a1, t0
19	    .option push
20	    .option norvc
21	    slli    x0, x0, 0x1f
22	    ebreak
23	    srai    x0, x0, 0x7
24	    .option pop
25	99: j       99b
26	.endm
27

content.text:
Before I copy/wire anything, I need clarity. The demo runtime targets the AndesCycle simulator's memory map:
- RAM at 0x0-..., sim_control MMIO at 0xE0080000 (matches vsim)
- `_exit()` writes EXIT_COMMAND to 0xE0080000 (vsim handles this; QEMU `-M virt` does NOT — it has no peripheral there)
- HTIF syscalls also go to 0xE0080010 (same story)
- `crt0.S` enables d-cache + d-cache coherence (mcache_ctl writes that QEMU virt's CPU may trap on)

Existing e2e fixtures (`hybrid/test/rt_*.S`) instead exit via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak` triple — that DOES work on both QEMU `-M virt -semihosting` and presumably on vsim's gdbstub bridge.

So the demo runtime as-is is **vsim-only**. It won't run on QEMU virt without either (a) adding a fake sim_control device to QEMU, or (b) replacing `_exit`/HTIF with semihosting in libgloss.

Three concrete options — which do you want?

**A. Copy runtime as-is, only target vsim** — link a `hello`-style C test that runs only under vsim. Skip QEMU. Smallest scope; matches vsim-demo verbatim.

**B. Copy + adapt `_exit`/`_write` to use semihosting** — single C-level e2e fixture runnable on both QEMU virt and vsim. Loses HTIF file syscalls (open/read/lseek...) on QEMU side, keeps them on vsim. Fits the round-trip flow.

**C. Both: keep HTIF version (vsim) AND a semihosting variant (QEMU+vsim)** — two libgloss flavors selected at link time.

Also: should the fixture still use the `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` drain markers (so it slots into the existing `roundtrip_e2e.sh` flow), or is this a fresh non-round-trip path?

message.content: I don't know. The purpose is for hybrid simulator to run.

3. Add a proper linker script (the existing `handoff_roundtrip.ld` discards `.data`/`.bss` — won't work for C)
4. Add a C-level e2e fixture (`rt_c_hello.c`) with the same `csrwi 0x7C0,0` / `csrwi 0x7C0,1` drain markers
5. Wire it into `hybrid/test/Makefile` and `roundtrip_e2e.sh`'s fixture list, then run.

content.text:
## 1. Why vsim needs those files

vsim is bare-metal. It just loads your ELF, jumps to entry, runs. It does NOT provide a boot ROM, libc, trap vector, or syscall layer. So the ELF itself must carry all of that:

| File | What it provides | Why vsim needs it |
|---|---|---|
| `crt0.S` | `_start` entry point | Initializes caches (mcache_ctl), stack pointer (`_stack`), global pointer, FP/Vector enable bits (mstatus.fs/vs), unaligned-access bit (mmisc_ctl.UNA), zeroes `.bss`, calls `init_mtvec`, then `main`, then `_exit`. Without this, you can't link a C program at all (`-nostartfiles` removes newlib's default crt0). |
| `trap.S` | `m_trap_entry` | Saves x1..x31 + mepc/mstatus/mxstatus, calls `handle_trap`, restores, `mret`. Any exception (ecall, illegal-instr, fault) without this just re-traps forever or hangs. |
| `handler.c` | `handle_trap`, `init_mtvec`, syscall dispatch | Constructor `init_mtvec` writes `mtvec=&m_trap_entry`. `handle_trap` routes M-mode ECALL to libgloss's `_open/_read/_write/...`; everything else dumps regs and `_exit`s. |
| `libgloss.c` | newlib backend stubs (`_write`, `_read`, `_open`, `_close`, `_exit`, `_sbrk`, `_fstat`, ...) | Newlib is freestanding — `printf`/`fopen`/etc bottom out into these stubs. The demo's stubs forward to vsim's MMIO `sim_control` peripheral: `_write` pokes `0xE008_0004` (putchar) or HTIF `0xE008_0010`; `_exit` writes the exit code to `0xE008_0000`; `_sbrk` walks from the linker `_end` symbol. vsim's `src/platform/sim_control.hpp` is the matching peer that decodes those writes. |

So: vsim needs them because vsim provides no runtime of its own — `crt0` brings up the hart, `trap.S` catches faults, `handler.c` dispatches them, and `libgloss.c` is the bridge between newlib and vsim's MMIO syscall peripheral.

## 2. Why these files can run on QEMU — partially

**Short answer: the round-trip flow only ever runs the *vsim-agnostic* prefix of these files on QEMU. The vsim-specific MMIO bits (`_exit`, `_write`, HTIF) are never reached on QEMU.**

What's actually portable across QEMU `-M virt` and vsim:
- All standard RISC-V instructions in `crt0.S`/`trap.S`.
- Andes-custom CSRs (`mcache_ctl 0x7CA`, `mmisc_ctl 0x7D0`, `mxstatus 0x7C4`, `mdcause 0x7C9`, `mucontrol 0x7C0`) — these only work because the user's QEMU at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` is the **Andes-patched fork** that recognizes them. Stock QEMU would trap. The fact that today's `rt_*.S` fixtures already use `csrwi 0x7C0, 0/1` and pass under QEMU 1 confirms this.
- Memory layout: `handoff_roundtrip.ld` puts `.text` at `0x80000000`, which is exactly QEMU virt's RAM base AND inside vsim's `RAM_BASE..RAM_END (0x0..0x8FFFFFFF)`. Same ELF loads in both.

What's NOT portable — and why the round-trip flow dodges it:
- `libgloss._exit()` writes `0xE008_0000`. QEMU virt has no device there → the write is silently dropped/fault and the program never terminates cleanly.
- `libgloss._write()` to stdout pokes `0xE008_0004`. Same story on QEMU.
- HTIF syscalls write `0xE008_0010`. Same story on QEMU.

The hybrid round-trip avoids all three because each phase only runs a *slice* of the program:
- **QEMU 1**: runs `crt0` → `main` until `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. The hybrid TCG plugin recognizes that CSR write, drains GPR/PC state, and **kills QEMU before any libgloss code executes**.
- **vsim**: resumes from drained state, runs the body, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`. vsim's handoff controller drains again and exits 200 — `_exit` is also never called here.
- **QEMU 2**: vsim spawns it with `-semihosting`. QEMU 2 resumes after the second marker and exits via the RISC-V semihosting `ebreak` triple (the `SEMIHOST_EXIT` macro), not via `libgloss._exit`. That's why the existing `rt_*.S` fixtures all hand-roll the semihosting exit instead of calling `_exit`.

**Bottom line:** these files run on QEMU only because QEMU never reaches their MMIO-dependent code. To run a C-level test (with `printf`, `exit(0)` etc) end-to-end on QEMU, you'd need to either (a) replace `_exit`/`_write` with semihosting, or (b) teach QEMU about a fake sim_control peripheral. The demo runtime itself is fundamentally an Andes-simulator runtime.

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the report:

## Summary of Hybrid Simulator Round-Trip Fixture

### 1. **Canonical Tier-1 Fixture (handoff_roundtrip.S/ld)**

**Phases:**
- **Phase 1 (QEMU1 entry):** Executes `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` at `_start` (line 13:S). Plugin detects drain marker, saves all GPRs/FPRs/CSRs/PMPs/vector state to binary file, exits QEMU with code 0.
- **Phase 2 (vsim resume):** Loads drained state from file via gdbstub, restores into vCPU, executes `li a0,1; li a1,2; add a2,a0,a1` (line 14-16:S), computing a2=3 within vsim only.
- **Phase 3 (vsim exit):** Executes `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` (line 17:S). HandoffController detects exit trigger, drains fresh GPRs+PC (merges preserved FP/CSR from QEMU1 snapshot via passthrough), exits vsim with code 200.
- **Phase 4 (QEMU2 handback):** Spawns new QEMU2 with `-gdb tcp::PORT -S -semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, restores all state via gdbstub, continues execution.
- **Phase 5 (QEMU2 exit):** Executes semihosting exit triple (`slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` from semihost_exit.inc:21-23), exits with a2 (3) as process status.

**Entry symbol:** `_start` (handoff_roundtrip.ld:4)  
**Load address:** `0x80000000` (handoff_roundtrip.ld:7; matches QEMU virt machine RAM base)  
**Sections:** `.text` survives; `/DISCARD/ : { *(.*) }` (line 9:ld) discards all other sections (metadata, debug, etc.)

---

### 2. **QEMU Plugin (hybrid_handoff.c)**

**CSR 0x7C0 Recognition:**  
The plugin uses `hybrid_match_handoff_insn()` (insn_match.h:26–51) which pattern-matches the instruction:
- Opcode must be SYSTEM (0x73) with rd=x0
- CSR field must equal 0x7C0 (insn_match.h:22, 31)
- For `csrwi` (funct3=0b101), uimm=0→ENTER, uimm=1→EXIT (insn_match.h:34–47)

**Termination:** On match, `on_handoff()` (hybrid_handoff.c:171–240) reads all 32 GPRs, 32 FPRs (if advertised), M-mode CSRs, PMP config/addr slots, and vector regs (if vlenb>0). Atomically writes `struct hybrid_state_v1` to file (write_state_atomic:144–169), logs PC, and calls `exit(0)`.

**ELF requirements:** None; the plugin operates at TCG instruction granularity via qemu_plugin_tb_trans (vcpu_tb_trans:242–259). It scans every 4-byte TB and does not require symbols or specific memory layout.

---

### 3. **Semihosting Flags (qemu_handback.hpp)**

**QEMU2 spawn:** Line 131–132:
```cpp
argv.push_back("-semihosting-config");
argv.push_back("enable=on,target=native");
```
Target is `native` (host syscall forwarding), not `guest`.

**vsim's own semihosting:** vsim does NOT execute semihosting ebreak itself. The handoff_controller (handoff_controller.hpp:45–47) fires on HYBRID_HANDOFF_EXIT and drains state; the caller (simulator.hpp, not shown here) writes the file and returns exit code 200. vsim trusts QEMU2 to execute the `ebreak` triple and terminate with the correct exit status.

---

### 4. **Platform HTIF_PUTCHAR (sim_control.hpp)**

**Address:** `0xE0000000 + HTIF_PUTCHAR_OFFSET` = `0xE0000004` (sim_control.hpp:21, 138)

**Write handling (line 138–139):**
```cpp
} else if (offset == HTIF_PUTCHAR_OFFSET && len == 1) {
  std::putchar(buf[0]);
```
Accepts **1-byte writes only**; calls `std::putchar()` on the byte. Order is little-endian unpack (sim_control.hpp:205–212), but for a single byte this is trivial.

---

### 5. **Toolchain at /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/**

**Binaries present:** YES  
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc` → symlink to `riscv64-elf-gcc` (14.2.0, 2025-03-27_nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d-c369da5c1)  
- Full nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d libc/newlib

**Flags (Makefile line 9):**
- `-march=rv64gc` (RV64I + Multiply/Divide + Atomic + Compressed)
- `-mabi=lp64d` (LP64 with double-precision floating point)

---

### 6. **CSR 0x7C0 (mucontrol) in /local/nick/qemu_v5**

**Found:** YES  
QEMU's Andes patch defines CSR 0x7C0 as `CSR_MILMB` ("M-mode I-cache Local Memory Base", andes_cpu_bits.h:29). The vendored QEMU has Andes-specific CSR support (csr_andes.c/h) and does NOT trap on 0x7C0; the plugin's pattern match relies on this CSR being recognized by QEMU's decoder so the instruction retires and the plugin callback fires.

input.content:
# C-level hello-world e2e fixture for the hybrid simulator

## Context

Today's hybrid round-trip e2e fixtures (`hybrid/test/rt_*.S`) are bare-metal RV64 assembly. They cannot call `printf`, `malloc`, or any newlib API; their linker script (`handoff_roundtrip.ld`) discards `.data`, `.bss`, `.rodata`. This blocks writing realistic e2e tests that exercise the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 hand-off through C-level code.

The vsim-demo project (`/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo`) already has a working C runtime targeting the AndesCycle simulator: `crt0.S`, `trap.S`, `handler.c`, `libgloss.c`. vsim's `src/platform/sim_control.hpp` peripheral at `0xE0080000` matches the demo's HTIF/exit MMIO contract, so the runtime drops into vsim verbatim.

The runtime is **not** drop-in for QEMU `-M virt` because libgloss's `_exit` and `_write` write to `0xE0080000`/`0xE0080004` and QEMU virt has no peripheral there. In our round-trip flow, QEMU1 never reaches `_exit` (the plugin kills it at `csrwi 0x7C0,0`) and `_write` is never reached either if all `printf`s sit between the two drain markers (only vsim runs that span). The only QEMU-side reachable libgloss path is QEMU2's terminal `_exit`, which we redirect to RISC-V semihosting (QEMU2 is spawned with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, per `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:131`).

Goal: stand up `rt_c_hello.elf` -- a C-level fixture that runs through the full round-trip and emits "hello from vsim" during the vsim phase.

## Files to add

### `hybrid/test/runtime/` (already populated verbatim from vsim-demo)

Verbatim copies (keep as-is):
- `crt0.S` -- entry, cache/FP/V/UNA bring-up, bss clear, calls `init_mtvec` then `main` then `_exit`. `_stack` is weak.
- `trap.S` -- `m_trap_entry`, GPR save/restore, calls `handle_trap`.
- `handler.c` -- `handle_trap`, `init_mtvec` (writes `mtvec=&m_trap_entry`), syscall dispatch table.
- `htif.h`, `syscall.h`, `core_v5.h`, `platform.h`, `memory_map.h` -- headers only.

One file modified:
- `libgloss.c` -- replace `_exit` with a semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED` (reason `0x20026`, ebreak triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Pattern is identical to `hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc:12-26`, just expressed as inline asm in C with the parameter block in `.bss` instead of at fixed `0x80001000`. Keep `_write` writing to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR=0xE0080004` so vsim's `sim_control` (`src/platform/sim_control.hpp:138`, accepts 1-byte writes -> `std::putchar`) prints it. QEMU2 never executes `_write` because `printf` is gated to the vsim-only span between the two markers.

### `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` (new)

Linker script that, unlike `handoff_roundtrip.ld`, preserves `.rodata`/`.data`/`.bss` and exposes `_edata`/`_end` (consumed by `crt0.S`'s bss clear) and `_stack`:

```
ENTRY(_start)
SECTIONS {
    . = 0x80000000;
    .text : { *(.text*) }
    .rodata : { *(.rodata*) *(.srodata*) }
    .data : { *(.data*) *(.sdata*) }
    _edata = .;
    .bss : { *(.bss*) *(.sbss*) *(COMMON) }
    _end = .;
    PROVIDE(_stack = 0x80700000);  /* within QEMU virt's default 128 MiB and within vsim RAM (0x0..0x8FFFFFFF) */
}
```

### `hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.c` (new)

```c
#include <stdio.h>

int main(void) {
    asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 0");           /* QEMU1 drains here */
    printf("hello from vsim\n");              /* only vsim runs this span */
    asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 1");           /* vsim drains, exits 200 */
    return 0;                                 /* QEMU2 -> _exit -> semihosting */
}
```

## Files to modify

### `hybrid/test/Makefile`

Add a `rt_c_hello.elf` target. Build with newlib (don't pass `-nostdlib`); pass `-nostartfiles` and link runtime sources:

```make
RUNTIME_DIR := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
               -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

rt_c_hello.elf: rt_c_hello.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	      rt_c_hello.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) -o $@
```

Also extend `all:` and `clean:` to include `rt_c_hello.elf`.

### `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`

Append one line to `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES` (around line 95):

```cmake
"rt_c_hello:0"
```

This auto-registers `test_e2e_rt_c_hello` via the existing foreach, reusing `roundtrip_e2e.sh` unchanged.

## Why this is safe across both halves

- **QEMU1 phase** -- runs `_start` (crt0 cache/FP/V setup), `init_mtvec`, into `main`, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Andes-patched QEMU at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` recognizes CSR `0x7C0` (existing fixtures rely on this). The plugin's `on_handoff()` drains all GPR/FPR/CSR/PMP/V state and `exit(0)`s. `_exit`/`_write` are never reached.
- **vsim phase** -- gdbstub-restores state, executes `printf` (newlib -> `_write` -> 1-byte writes to `0xE0080004` -> `sim_control` prints to vsim's stdout), hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`. `HandoffController` drains, vsim exits 200 (matches existing flow). `_exit` not reached.
- **QEMU2 phase** -- spawned by vsim with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, gdbstub-restored, falls through `return 0`, crt0 calls `_exit(0)`, our patched `_exit` issues the semihosting exit triple, QEMU2 terminates with status `0`. vsim propagates that as its own exit code.

`_stack=0x80700000` sits in both QEMU virt RAM (default 128 MiB at `0x80000000`) and vsim RAM (`RAM_BASE..RAM_END` = `0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

## Verification

End-to-end:

```bash
# Build the fixture
make -C /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_hello.elf
# Confirm vsim and QEMU plugin are built
ls /local/nick/vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium \
   /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
# Run the round-trip via the existing harness
cd /local/nick/vsim/build && \
  FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh
```

Expected output: `[rt_c_hello 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> ...`, `[rt_c_hello 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2` (vsim-side log will contain `hello from vsim`), `[rt_c_hello 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code 0`.

After CMake reconfigure, `ctest -R test_e2e_rt_c_hello` runs the same path under CTest with the same `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` semantics.

Spot checks if anything fails:
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump -h hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.elf` -- confirm `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss` present, all loaded near `0x80000000`.
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-nm hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.elf | grep -E '_start|_stack|_end|_edata|main'` -- symbols defined.
- Run vsim alone first with `--shared-mem-base` etc, confirm `printf` byte stream lands in stdout.

## Critical files

- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c` (modify `_exit`)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` (new)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.c` (new)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (add target)
- `/local/nick/vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (register fixture)

Reused unchanged:
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{crt0.S,trap.S,handler.c,htif.h,syscall.h,core_v5.h,platform.h,memory_map.h}`
- `/local/nick/vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh` (parameterized by FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC)
- `/local/nick/vsim/src/platform/sim_control.hpp` (the MMIO peer for libgloss)
- `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (already passes `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)

- **QEMU2 phase** -- spawned by vsim with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, gdbstub-restored, falls through `return 0`, crt0 calls `_exit(0)`, our patched `_exit` issues the semihosting exit triple, QEMU2 terminates with status `0`. vsim propagates that as its own exit code.

`_stack=0x80700000` sits in both QEMU virt RAM (default 128 MiB at `0x80000000`) and vsim RAM (`RAM_BASE..RAM_END` = `0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

1. When vsim hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` and decides to spawn QEMU2, what state is captured? GPRs, FPRs, CSRs, PMP, vector regs -- yes. But what about MEMORY? Is RAM contents transferred to QEMU2, or does QEMU2 get a fresh `-kernel ELF` load (i.e., .text/.rodata/.data reset, .bss zeroed, stack untouched)?

2. The QEMU2 spawn argv -- does it pass `-kernel <ELF>` (which would reload memory from ELF) and ALSO somehow override RAM contents from a vsim-produced state file? Or only registers via gdbstub?

3. Specifically, if a C program in vsim writes to its stack at sp=0x80700000 (so writes RAM at e.g. 0x806FFFE0), and then hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, can QEMU2 read that same byte at 0x806FFFE0 after the handoff? Or is 0x806FFFE0 zeroed/uninit on QEMU2?

4. Same question for `.bss` and `.data` sections. If vsim modifies a `static int` in `.data`, does QEMU2 see the modification?

content.text:
Perfect. Now I have all the information I need. Let me compile the precise answer:

---

## Precise Analysis: Memory State Transfer in vsim→QEMU2 Handoff

**Q1: What state is captured when csrwi 0x7C0, 1 triggers handoff?**

From `qemu_handback.hpp:256-316` and `state_drain.hpp:29-61`: vsim captures only **architectural registers** — x1..x31, PC, FPRs, M-mode CSRs (mstatus, mepc, mtvec, mcause, mtval, mscratch, medeleg, mideleg, mip, mie, mcycle, minstret, satp), all 16 PMP CFG slots + 64 PMP ADDR slots, and vector registers (vstart, vtype, vl, vlenb, v0..v31). **MEMORY IS NOT CAPTURED** in the state file. The `hybrid_state_v1` struct (state_abi.h) contains zero fields for RAM content — it is registers-only (1904 bytes).

**Q2 & Q3: QEMU2 argv and RAM persistence?**

From `qemu_handback.hpp:102-146`, QEMU is always spawned with `-kernel <ELF>` (line 133-134). This causes QEMU's firmware to **reload the ELF from disk**, which resets .text, .rodata, .data to ELF values and zeros .bss. **Stack modifications made in vsim (writes to 0x806FFFE0) are NOT preserved** — QEMU2's loaded .bss and stack region are virgin, not inherited from vsim's heap/stack writes.

**Q4: .data and .bss modifications?**

If vsim modifies a `static int` in .data before handoff, that modification **is lost**. QEMU2 reloads .data from the ELF file on disk, overwriting any runtime changes vsim made.

**Q5: Tier-2 shared-mem flow changes this entirely.**

Looking at `qemu_handback.hpp:102-146`, when `shared_mem_path` is non-empty (Tier-2 mode):
- QEMU is spawned with `-object memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=<PATH>,size=<SIZE>` and `-machine virt,memory-backend=mem` (lines 114-123).
- This mmap'd file is **shared between vsim and QEMU2** — both processes see the same RAM bytes.
- **vsim's stack writes at 0x806FFFE0 ARE visible in QEMU2** and QEMU2's ELF reload does NOT overwrite the shared mmap region — QEMU loads the kernel but reads from the mmap'd backing (zero-copy handoff).

From `main.cpp:302-321`: Tier-1 fixtures (rt_pc_jump, rt_all_gprs) do **NOT** use `--shared-mem-path` or `--shared-mem-size`, so they rely **solely on register-state carry-over via gdbstub** (lines 273-316 in qemu_handback.hpp). They cannot preserve stack variables.

**Bottom line for C-level fixtures:** Tier-1 requires return values in **callee-saved registers only** (a5..a7, s0..s11). Tier-2 with `--shared-mem-path` can use stack-resident return values since RAM survives the boundary intact.

+# C-level e2e test suite for the hybrid simulator

-Today's hybrid round-trip e2e fixtures (`hybrid/test/rt_*.S`) are bare-metal RV64 assembly. They cannot call `printf`, `malloc`, or any newlib API; their linker script (`handoff_roundtrip.ld`) discards `.data`, `.bss`, `.rodata`. This blocks writing realistic e2e tests that exercise the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 hand-off through C-level code.

+`hybrid/test/rt_*.S` are bare RV64 assembly. Their linker script (`handoff_roundtrip.ld:9`) discards `.data`/`.bss`/`.rodata` and the test bodies hand-roll the semihosting exit. This blocks any test that exercises C runtime paths -- `printf` formatting, newlib's stdio, HTIF file I/O.

-The vsim-demo project (`/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo`) already has a working C runtime targeting the AndesCycle simulator: `crt0.S`, `trap.S`, `handler.c`, `libgloss.c`. vsim's `src/platform/sim_control.hpp` peripheral at `0xE0080000` matches the demo's HTIF/exit MMIO contract, so the runtime drops into vsim verbatim.

+vsim-demo (`/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo`) ships a working C runtime targeting the AndesCycle simulator: `crt0.S`, `trap.S`, `handler.c`, `libgloss.c`. vsim's `src/platform/sim_control.hpp` peripheral at `0xE0080000` matches the demo's HTIF/exit MMIO contract, so the runtime drops in verbatim. `libgloss.c` exits via writing to `0xE0080000`; vsim's `handle_sim_command` (`src/platform/sim_control.hpp:160-203`) decodes it. `libgloss.c` does file I/O via writing the syscall packet address to `0xE0080010`; vsim's `dispatch` (`sim_control.hpp:249-277`) routes through `sim_syscall_table[]` to host syscalls.

-The runtime is **not** drop-in for QEMU `-M virt` because libgloss's `_exit` and `_write` write to `0xE0080000`/`0xE0080004` and QEMU virt has no peripheral there. In our round-trip flow, QEMU1 never reaches `_exit` (the plugin kills it at `csrwi 0x7C0,0`) and `_write` is never reached either if all `printf`s sit between the two drain markers (only vsim runs that span). The only QEMU-side reachable libgloss path is QEMU2's terminal `_exit`, which we redirect to RISC-V semihosting (QEMU2 is spawned with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, per `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:131`).
+Goal: stand up three C-level fixtures runnable through the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 round-trip:
+- `rt_c_file_io` -- HTIF file I/O round-trip (`fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink`) with self-validation
-Goal: stand up `rt_c_hello.elf` -- a C-level fixture that runs through the full round-trip and emits "hello from vsim" during the vsim phase.
+| QEMU1        | none -- `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` first  | QEMU virt has no `0xE0080000`; plugin kills QEMU at the marker before any I/O |

+| vsim         | `_write` (any fd), `_open`, `_read`, `_close`, `_lseek`, `_fstat`, `_unlink`, `_sbrk`, `_gettimeofday` | vsim's `sim_control` handles them as MMIO putchar (fd<=2) or HTIF syscall (fd>2 / file ops) |
+| QEMU2        | `_exit` only (via semihosting)  | QEMU virt has no `0xE0080000`; semihosting is the only termination path |

+Critical state-transfer fact (verified against `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` and `src/hybrid/state_drain.hpp`): in **Tier-1** (no `--shared-mem-path`) only architectural registers cross the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- GPRs, FPRs, M-mode CSRs, PMP, V regs. **RAM does not.** QEMU2 is spawned with `-kernel <ELF>` so `.text`/`.rodata`/`.data` reload from disk and `.bss`/stack are virgin. Any value computed in vsim that must reach QEMU2's `_exit` has to live in a callee-saved register or `a0` at the moment of `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`.
+All three fixtures avoid the issue by structuring main() so:
+- The success path returns a compile-time-constant 0; compiler emits `li a0, 0; ret` after the marker, which works regardless of memory state.

+- The failure path bypasses the round-trip entirely by writing `SimCommand::FAILED` (`0x01234569`) directly to `0xE0080000`, causing vsim to terminate with exit code 1 before any handoff. The harness's `EXPECT_RC=0` mismatch flags the failure.
-- `crt0.S` -- entry, cache/FP/V/UNA bring-up, bss clear, calls `init_mtvec` then `main` then `_exit`. `_stack` is weak.
+- `crt0.S` -- entry, cache/FP/V/UNA bring-up, `.bss` clear, calls `init_mtvec` then `main` then `_exit`. `_stack` is weak.

-- `libgloss.c` -- replace `_exit` with a semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED` (reason `0x20026`, ebreak triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Pattern is identical to `hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc:12-26`, just expressed as inline asm in C with the parameter block in `.bss` instead of at fixed `0x80001000`. Keep `_write` writing to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR=0xE0080004` so vsim's `sim_control` (`src/platform/sim_control.hpp:138`, accepts 1-byte writes -> `std::putchar`) prints it. QEMU2 never executes `_write` because `printf` is gated to the vsim-only span between the two markers.
+Patch `libgloss.c` `_exit` to a **dual-path exit** -- vsim wins first on its platform, semihosting wins on QEMU virt:
+  /* vsim path: sim_control treats command < 256 as raw exit code.
+     On QEMU virt this write is silently dropped (unassigned MMIO). */
+  /* QEMU virt path (also QEMU2 in our flow): RISC-V semihosting
+     SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED. Same ebreak triple as hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc:21-23. */
+The dual path also rescues the case where `handler.c`'s `exception_handler` calls `_exit(mcause)` after a trap during the vsim phase -- without it, semihosting alone would hang vsim.

+    PROVIDE(_stack = 0x80700000);   /* in QEMU virt's default 128 MiB and in vsim RAM (0x0..0x8FFFFFFF) */
+/* Vsim-only failure: writes SimCommand::FAILED to sim_control,
+   vsim exits with code 1, harness sees RC=1 vs EXPECT_RC=0. */
+Smoke: round-trip, printf in vsim phase, exit 0.
+    setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);          /* unbuffered: every byte hits 0xE0080004 immediately */

+## Why this works on both halves

-- **QEMU1 phase** -- runs `_start` (crt0 cache/FP/V setup), `init_mtvec`, into `main`, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Andes-patched QEMU at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` recognizes CSR `0x7C0` (existing fixtures rely on this). The plugin's `on_handoff()` drains all GPR/FPR/CSR/PMP/V state and `exit(0)`s. `_exit`/`_write` are never reached.

-- **vsim phase** -- gdbstub-restores state, executes `printf` (newlib -> `_write` -> 1-byte writes to `0xE0080004` -> `sim_control` prints to vsim's stdout), hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`. `HandoffController` drains, vsim exits 200 (matches existing flow). `_exit` not reached.

-- **QEMU2 phase** -- spawned by vsim with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, gdbstub-restored, falls through `return 0`, crt0 calls `_exit(0)`, our patched `_exit` issues the semihosting exit triple, QEMU2 terminates with status `0`. vsim propagates that as its own exit code.

+- **QEMU1**: `crt0.S` brings up caches/FP/V/UNA, `init_mtvec` writes `mtvec`, `main` runs the prologue (no I/O), hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Plugin's `on_handoff` (`hybrid/qemu_plugin/hybrid_handoff.c:171-240`) drains and `exit(0)`s QEMU. No libgloss reachable -> no `0xE0080000` access -> safe on QEMU virt.

+- **vsim**: gdbstub-restores GPR/PC/FP/CSR, runs the body. `printf` -> newlib -> `_write(1, ...)` -> per-byte poke to `0xE0080004`, picked up by `sim_control.hpp:138`. `fopen`/`fwrite`/`fread`/`fclose`/`unlink` -> `frontend_syscall` -> packet at `magicmem`, address written to `0xE0080010`, vsim's `dispatch` (`sim_control.hpp:249-277`) executes the host syscall and writes back the return value. `setvbuf(_IONBF)` ensures every printf byte hits the wire before `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`. On any mismatch: `rt_fail()` writes `0x01234569` to `0xE0080000`, vsim's `handle_sim_command` (`sim_control.hpp:188`) sets `exits=1`, `simulator.hpp:399-404` returns 1 before handoff. On success: `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` triggers handoff, vsim spawns QEMU2.

+- **QEMU2**: gdbstub-restored, falls through `return 0` (compiler emits `li a0, 0; ret`, then `crt0.S` does `call _exit`). Patched `_exit` writes `0xE0080000` first (silent dead on QEMU virt) then issues semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED 0`. QEMU2 (spawned with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native` per `qemu_handback.hpp:131`) terminates with status 0; vsim's `waitpid` returns it; vsim's process exit = 0. Harness sees `RC == EXPECT_RC == 0` -> PASS.
-`_stack=0x80700000` sits in both QEMU virt RAM (default 128 MiB at `0x80000000`) and vsim RAM (`RAM_BASE..RAM_END` = `0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).
+Stack `_stack=0x80700000` is in QEMU virt's default 128 MiB RAM (`0x80000000..0x88000000`) and in vsim RAM (`RAM_BASE..RAM_END = 0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`), so both halves' stack writes land in real RAM.

+- `rt_c_printf`: vsim log contains `dec=-42 hex=deadbeefcafe str=vsim chr=Z ptr=0x80000000 pad=[    7]` and the widths line; RC=0.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# C-level hello-world e2e fixture for the hybrid simulator

## Context

Today's hybrid round-trip e2e fixtures (`hybrid/test/rt_*.S`) are bare-metal RV64 assembly. They cannot call `printf`, `malloc`, or any newlib API; their linker script (`handoff_roundtrip.ld`) discards `.data`, `.bss`, `.rodata`. This blocks writing realistic e2e tests that exercise the full QEMU1 -> vsim -> QEMU2 hand-off through C-level code.

The vsim-demo project (`/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo`) already has a working C runtime targeting the AndesCycle simulator: `crt0.S`, `trap.S`, `handler.c`, `libgloss.c`. vsim's `src/platform/sim_control.hpp` peripheral at `0xE0080000` matches the demo's HTIF/exit MMIO contract, so the runtime drops into vsim verbatim.

The runtime is **not** drop-in for QEMU `-M virt` because libgloss's `_exit` and `_write` write to `0xE0080000`/`0xE0080004` and QEMU virt has no peripheral there. In our round-trip flow, QEMU1 never reaches `_exit` (the plugin kills it at `csrwi 0x7C0,0`) and `_write` is never reached either if all `printf`s sit between the two drain markers (only vsim runs that span). The only QEMU-side reachable libgloss path is QEMU2's terminal `_exit`, which we redirect to RISC-V semihosting (QEMU2 is spawned with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, per `src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp:131`).

Goal: stand up `rt_c_hello.elf` -- a C-level fixture that runs through the full round-trip and emits "hello from vsim" during the vsim phase.

## Files to add

### `hybrid/test/runtime/` (already populated verbatim from vsim-demo)

Verbatim copies (keep as-is):
- `crt0.S` -- entry, cache/FP/V/UNA bring-up, bss clear, calls `init_mtvec` then `main` then `_exit`. `_stack` is weak.
- `trap.S` -- `m_trap_entry`, GPR save/restore, calls `handle_trap`.
- `handler.c` -- `handle_trap`, `init_mtvec` (writes `mtvec=&m_trap_entry`), syscall dispatch table.
- `htif.h`, `syscall.h`, `core_v5.h`, `platform.h`, `memory_map.h` -- headers only.

One file modified:
- `libgloss.c` -- replace `_exit` with a semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED` (reason `0x20026`, ebreak triple `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7`). Pattern is identical to `hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc:12-26`, just expressed as inline asm in C with the parameter block in `.bss` instead of at fixed `0x80001000`. Keep `_write` writing to `DISPLAY_BASE_ADDR=0xE0080004` so vsim's `sim_control` (`src/platform/sim_control.hpp:138`, accepts 1-byte writes -> `std::putchar`) prints it. QEMU2 never executes `_write` because `printf` is gated to the vsim-only span between the two markers.

### `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` (new)

Linker script that, unlike `handoff_roundtrip.ld`, preserves `.rodata`/`.data`/`.bss` and exposes `_edata`/`_end` (consumed by `crt0.S`'s bss clear) and `_stack`:

```
ENTRY(_start)
SECTIONS {
    . = 0x80000000;
    .text : { *(.text*) }
    .rodata : { *(.rodata*) *(.srodata*) }
    .data : { *(.data*) *(.sdata*) }
    _edata = .;
    .bss : { *(.bss*) *(.sbss*) *(COMMON) }
    _end = .;
    PROVIDE(_stack = 0x80700000);  /* within QEMU virt's default 128 MiB and within vsim RAM (0x0..0x8FFFFFFF) */
}
```

### `hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.c` (new)

```c
#include <stdio.h>

int main(void) {
    asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 0");           /* QEMU1 drains here */
    printf("hello from vsim\n");              /* only vsim runs this span */
    asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 1");           /* vsim drains, exits 200 */
    return 0;                                 /* QEMU2 -> _exit -> semihosting */
}
```

## Files to modify

### `hybrid/test/Makefile`

Add a `rt_c_hello.elf` target. Build with newlib (don't pass `-nostdlib`); pass `-nostartfiles` and link runtime sources:

```make
RUNTIME_DIR := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
               -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

rt_c_hello.elf: rt_c_hello.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld \
	      rt_c_hello.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) -o $@
```

Also extend `all:` and `clean:` to include `rt_c_hello.elf`.

### `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake`

Append one line to `VSIM_RT_FIXTURES` (around line 95):

```cmake
"rt_c_hello:0"
```

This auto-registers `test_e2e_rt_c_hello` via the existing foreach, reusing `roundtrip_e2e.sh` unchanged.

## Why this is safe across both halves

- **QEMU1 phase** -- runs `_start` (crt0 cache/FP/V setup), `init_mtvec`, into `main`, hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`. Andes-patched QEMU at `/local/nick/qemu_v5` recognizes CSR `0x7C0` (existing fixtures rely on this). The plugin's `on_handoff()` drains all GPR/FPR/CSR/PMP/V state and `exit(0)`s. `_exit`/`_write` are never reached.
- **vsim phase** -- gdbstub-restores state, executes `printf` (newlib -> `_write` -> 1-byte writes to `0xE0080004` -> `sim_control` prints to vsim's stdout), hits `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`. `HandoffController` drains, vsim exits 200 (matches existing flow). `_exit` not reached.
- **QEMU2 phase** -- spawned by vsim with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, gdbstub-restored, falls through `return 0`, crt0 calls `_exit(0)`, our patched `_exit` issues the semihosting exit triple, QEMU2 terminates with status `0`. vsim propagates that as its own exit code.

`_stack=0x80700000` sits in both QEMU virt RAM (default 128 MiB at `0x80000000`) and vsim RAM (`RAM_BASE..RAM_END` = `0x0..0x8FFFFFFF`).

## Verification

End-to-end:

```bash
# Build the fixture
make -C /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test rt_c_hello.elf
# Confirm vsim and QEMU plugin are built
ls /local/nick/vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium \
   /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so
# Run the round-trip via the existing harness
cd /local/nick/vsim/build && \
  FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0 bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh
```

Expected output: `[rt_c_hello 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> ...`, `[rt_c_hello 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2` (vsim-side log will contain `hello from vsim`), `[rt_c_hello 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code 0`.

After CMake reconfigure, `ctest -R test_e2e_rt_c_hello` runs the same path under CTest with the same `SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77` semantics.

Spot checks if anything fails:
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump -h hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.elf` -- confirm `.text`, `.rodata`, `.data`, `.bss` present, all loaded near `0x80000000`.
- `riscv64-unknown-elf-nm hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.elf | grep -E '_start|_stack|_end|_edata|main'` -- symbols defined.
- Run vsim alone first with `--shared-mem-base` etc, confirm `printf` byte stream lands in stdout.

## Critical files

- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c` (modify `_exit`)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` (new)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.c` (new)
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/Makefile` (add target)
- `/local/nick/vsim/cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (register fixture)

Reused unchanged:
- `/local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/{crt0.S,trap.S,handler.c,htif.h,syscall.h,core_v5.h,platform.h,memory_map.h}`
- `/local/nick/vsim/tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh` (parameterized by FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC)
- `/local/nick/vsim/src/platform/sim_control.hpp` (the MMIO peer for libgloss)
- `/local/nick/vsim/src/hybrid/qemu_handback.hpp` (already passes `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`)

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:

	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:

	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

content.text: ELF builds and links cleanly. But there's an issue: `main` ended up at 0x80000000 (the load address) ahead of `_start` at 0x80000030. The entry-point header field still points to `_start` (which both QEMU `load_elf` and vsim's LIEF loader respect), but it's safer to put `_start` at the load address so any loader that ignores `e_entry` still does the right thing. Let me fix the linker script.

content.text: The wildcard `*crt0*` didn't match because gcc gives the assembled objects synthetic temp filenames. Simpler fix: put crt0.S first in the input order — within `*(.text*)` ld preserves input order.

input.old_string:
# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $< $(RUNTIME_SRCS) -o $@
input.new_string:
# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

input.new_string:
echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, mxstatus,
# uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that crt0/handler from the vsim-demo runtime touch.
# Default rv64 raises illegal-instruction on the very first mcache_ctl write
# in crt0, leaving QEMU spinning in re-trap (mtvec=0). Existing rt_*.S
# fixtures don't touch those CSRs but happily run under the Andes CPU too.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mp -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
# -cpu andes-ax45mp gates Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, mxstatus,
# uitb, jvt, mdcause, ...) that crt0/handler from the vsim-demo runtime touch.
# Default rv64 raises illegal-instruction on the very first mcache_ctl write

# fixtures don't touch those CSRs but happily run under the Andes CPU too.
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -cpu andes-ax45mp -bios none -nographic \

toolUseResult.originalFile:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# End-to-end roundtrip:
#   1. QEMU 1 + plugin: drains state at `csrwi 0x7C0, 0`.
#   2. vsim: resumes, runs body, drains at `csrwi 0x7C0, 1`, exits 200.
#   3. vsim's --qemu-binary path: spawns QEMU 2 with -gdb -S -semihosting,
#      restores GPRs+PC via gdbstub, continues.
#   4. QEMU 2 hits the RISC-V semihosting ebreak-triple, exits with the
#      semihosting exit_code (low 8 bits of a2 in the existing fixture).
#
# Fixture-parameterized:
#   FIXTURE     name of .elf under hybrid/test/  (default: handoff_roundtrip)
#   EXPECT_RC   expected vsim process exit code  (default: 3)
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN         path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU        path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN      path to libhybrid_handoff.so
#   VSIM_ELF         path to <fixture>.elf  (overrides FIXTURE if set)

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-handoff_roundtrip}"
EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-3}"

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77   # CTest skip code when SKIP_RETURN_CODE is set
  fi
done

STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1: drain entry state -> $STATE_QEMU"
"$VSIM_QEMU" -M virt -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim: resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

+# Default rv64 raises illegal-instruction on the very first mcache_ctl write
+# in crt0, leaving QEMU spinning in re-trap (mtvec=0). Existing rt_*.S

==> /local/nick/vsim/build-docker.sh <==
#!/bin/bash

# VSim Workspace Build Script
# This script builds released vsim image tar file

set -e

# arguments to docker targets
DOCKER_TARGET="$1"
if [[ ! "$DOCKER_TARGET" =~ ^(dev|runtime|bin|debug)$ ]]; then
    echo "Error: Invalid argument '$1'"
    echo "Usage: $0 {dev|runtime|bin|debug}"
    echo "  dev     - Development environment"
    echo "  runtime - Runtime environment"
    echo "  bin     - Binary only"
    echo "  debug   - debug version"
    exit 1
fi

# Generate a version file
tag=$(git describe --tags --always)
echo "vsim version : " "$tag"

if [[ "$DOCKER_TARGET" == "bin" ]]; then
    podman build --build-arg VERSION="$tag" --output=type=local,dest=andescycle-bin-"$tag" .
else
    # build image using docker or podman, archive build results
    podman build --target "$DOCKER_TARGET" --build-arg VERSION="$tag" -t andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" .
    podman save localhost/andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET":"$tag" | gzip > andescycle-"$DOCKER_TARGET"-"$tag".tar.gz
fi


echo "✅ Build complete"

content.content:
1	#!/usr/bin/env bash
2	# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
3	#
4	# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
5	# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
6	# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
7	#
8	# Required environment:
9	#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
10	#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
11	#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so
12	
13	set -euo pipefail
14	
15	SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
16	REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)
17	
18	FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
19	EXPECT_RC=0
20	
21	VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
22	VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"

file.content:
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Tier-2 round-trip e2e: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
#
# QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2 all map the same backing file. Phase A writes a
# pattern in QEMU 1; Phase B verifies + mutates in vsim; Phase C re-reads
# in QEMU 2. Memory must survive both handoffs.
#
# Required environment:
#   VSIM_BIN     path to sim_<cpu> binary (host-runnable)
#   VSIM_QEMU    path to qemu-system-riscv64
#   VSIM_PLUGIN  path to libhybrid_handoff.so

set -euo pipefail

SCRIPT_DIR=$(cd -- "$(dirname -- "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)
REPO_ROOT=$(cd -- "$SCRIPT_DIR/../.." && pwd)

FIXTURE=rt_shared_mem
EXPECT_RC=0

VSIM_BIN="${VSIM_BIN:-$REPO_ROOT/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium}"
VSIM_QEMU="${VSIM_QEMU:-/local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64}"
VSIM_PLUGIN="${VSIM_PLUGIN:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so}"
VSIM_ELF="${VSIM_ELF:-$REPO_ROOT/hybrid/test/${FIXTURE}.elf}"

for f in "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_QEMU" "$VSIM_PLUGIN" "$VSIM_ELF"; do
  if [ ! -e "$f" ]; then
    echo "skip: $f not built; run 'make' under hybrid/test and hybrid/qemu_plugin"
    exit 77
  fi
done

# 128 MB matches the QEMU virt machine default; vsim must use the same size.
SHARED_SIZE=$((128 * 1024 * 1024))
SHARED_MEM=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
STATE_QEMU=$(mktemp)
STATE_VSIM=$(mktemp)
SIM_LOG=$(mktemp)
trap 'rm -f "$SHARED_MEM" "$STATE_QEMU" "$STATE_VSIM" "$SIM_LOG"' EXIT

truncate -s "$SHARED_SIZE" "$SHARED_MEM"

echo "[$FIXTURE 1/3] QEMU 1 (shared mmap): drain entry state"
"$VSIM_QEMU" \
  -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED_MEM,size=$SHARED_SIZE" \
  -machine virt,memory-backend=mem \
  -bios none -nographic \
  -kernel "$VSIM_ELF" \
  -plugin "$VSIM_PLUGIN,outfile=$STATE_QEMU" >/dev/null 2>&1

if [ ! -s "$STATE_QEMU" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: QEMU 1 did not produce state"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 2/3] vsim (shared mmap): resume + drain + spawn QEMU 2"
set +e
timeout 60 "$VSIM_BIN" "$VSIM_ELF" \
  --resume-from-qemu "$STATE_QEMU" \
  --handoff-out "$STATE_VSIM" \
  --shared-mem-path "$SHARED_MEM" \
  --shared-mem-size "$SHARED_SIZE" \
  --qemu-binary "$VSIM_QEMU" >"$SIM_LOG" 2>&1
RC=$?
set -e

if [ "$RC" != "$EXPECT_RC" ]; then
  echo "FAIL[$FIXTURE]: expected exit $EXPECT_RC, got $RC"
  echo "--- sim log ---"
  cat "$SIM_LOG"
  exit 1
fi

echo "[$FIXTURE 3/3] PASS: round-trip exit code $RC"

content.content:
1	// rt_shared_mem.S
2	//
3	// Tier-2 fixture: cross-simulator memory survival via shared mmap.
4	// Unlike Tier-1 fixtures, all three phases see the same backing file via
5	// QEMU's `-object memory-backend-file,share=on` and vsim's
6	// `--shared-mem-path`. Memory written in Phase A is visible in Phase B; the
7	// page Phase B mutates is visible in Phase C.
8	//
9	// Phase A (QEMU1, shared mmap): fill 4 KB at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5..A5.
10	// Phase B (vsim, same mmap):
11	//   1. read back; first mismatch -> exit 50.
12	//   2. XOR every word with 0x5A5A..5A in place; result is all-FF.
13	// Phase C (QEMU2, same mmap):
14	//   1. verify all 4 KB == 0xFF; first mismatch -> exit 51.
15	//   2. exit 0 on PASS.
16	//
17	// Distinct exit codes (50, 51) avoid the silent-pass aliasing that a
18	// running mismatch counter would have when (count mod 256) lands on 0.
19	
20	#include "semihost_exit.inc"
21	
22	.section .text
23	.globl _start
24	_start:
25	    // Phase A: fill 512 doublewords at 0x80010000 with 0xA5A5...A5.
26	    li      t0, 0x80010000
27	    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
28	    li      t1, 512
29	1:
30	    sd      t3, 0(t0)
31	    addi    t0, t0, 8
32	    addi    t1, t1, -1
33	    bnez    t1, 1b
34	
35	    csrwi   0x7C0, 0                        // QEMU1 drains, exits
36	
37	    // Phase B: verify Phase A pattern, XOR with 0x5A...5A in place.
38	    li      t0, 0x80010000
39	    li      t3, 0xA5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5
40	    li      t4, 0x5A5A5A5A5A5A5A5A
41	    li      t1, 512
42	verify_a:
43	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
44	    bne     t2, t3, fail_b
45	    addi    t0, t0, 8
46	    addi    t1, t1, -1
47	    bnez    t1, verify_a
48	
49	    li      t0, 0x80010000
50	    li      t1, 512
51	xor_loop:
52	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
53	    xor     t2, t2, t4
54	    sd      t2, 0(t0)
55	    addi    t0, t0, 8
56	    addi    t1, t1, -1
57	    bnez    t1, xor_loop
58	
59	    csrwi   0x7C0, 1                        // vsim drains, exits 200
60	
61	phase_c:
62	    // Phase C: verify all 4 KB == 0xFFFF...FF through the shared mmap.
63	    li      t0, 0x80010000
64	    li      t5, -1                          // sign-extends to 0xFFFF...FF
65	    li      t1, 512
66	verify_c:
67	    ld      t2, 0(t0)
68	    bne     t2, t5, fail_c
69	    addi    t0, t0, 8
70	    addi    t1, t1, -1
71	    bnez    t1, verify_c
72	
73	    li      a2, 0
74	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
75	
76	fail_b:
77	    li      a2, 50
78	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
79	
80	fail_c:
81	    li      a2, 51
82	    SEMIHOST_EXIT a2
83

lines: -    // Match QEMU 1's CPU type (see tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh): the Andes
-    // CPU gates the custom CSRs (mcache_ctl, mmisc_ctl, mxstatus, uitb, ...)
-    // that crt0/handler from the vsim-demo C runtime touch.

content.text: Let me back out the `qemu_handback.hpp` change (needs vsim rebuild) and instead use **Tier-2 shared mem** + **strip Andes-only CSR writes from crt0**. That solves both the cache-CSR illegal-instruction trap on QEMU 2 AND the stack-survival problem on the vsim->QEMU 2 boundary.

content.subject: Patch libgloss._exit to dual-path (sim_control + semihosting)
content.description: Edit /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c so _exit first writes EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR (vsim path) then issues the RISC-V semihosting SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED ebreak triple (QEMU virt path). Keep _write/HTIF paths as-is.

content.subject: Add rt_c.ld linker script + rt_c_helpers.h
content.description: Create /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld (preserves .data/.bss/.rodata, defines _edata/_end/_stack=0x80700000) and rt_c_helpers.h (drain markers + rt_fail).

content.subject: Register rt_c_hello in CMake and run e2e
content.description: Append "rt_c_hello:0" to VSIM_RT_FIXTURES in cmake/HybridConfig.cmake. Run roundtrip_e2e.sh with FIXTURE=rt_c_hello EXPECT_RC=0. Confirm RC=0 and "hello from vsim" appears in vsim's stdout log.

content.subject: Add rt_c_hello.c + Makefile rule
content.description: Create the smallest C-level fixture and wire it into hybrid/test/Makefile. Verify objdump shows .text/.rodata/.data/.bss at 0x80000000+ and _start/_stack/_end symbols are defined.

content.subject: Add rt_c_printf and verify e2e
content.description: Add rt_c_printf.c (format-specifier coverage), Makefile rule (auto via $(RT_C_FIXTURES) list), CMake registration. Run e2e and confirm RC=0 and expected formatted output appears in log.

content.subject: Add rt_c_file_io and verify e2e
content.description: Add rt_c_file_io.c (fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink with self-validation via rt_fail on mismatch). Wire it. Run e2e: confirm RC=0, "41 bytes round-tripped" in log, /tmp/rt_c_file_io.tmp absent. Then sanity-check failure path: corrupt the payload and confirm harness reports "expected exit 0, got 1".

content.text: Reverted. Now let me parameterize `rt_shared_mem.sh` to accept FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC env vars (preserving current behavior as default), then register the C fixtures via that harness.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
    "rt_c_hello:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_shared_mem
    COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh)
  set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_shared_mem PROPERTIES
    SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
    LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
  )

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

lines: -  /* DIAGNOSTIC: vsim-path write disabled to isolate whether unassigned-MMIO
-     writes cause QEMU virt to hang. Re-enable behind a vsim-vs-qemu detect. */
+  /* In the hybrid round-trip flow, _exit is only reached in QEMU 2 (vsim
+     itself terminates at the csrwi 0x7C0, 1 drain marker, well before main
+     SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED reaches the host with the right status. The ebreak
-  /* QEMU virt path: RISC-V semihosting SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED. Same ebreak triple as
-     hybrid/test/semihost_exit.inc. Block lives in .bss, not at fixed 0x80001000. */
+     We deliberately do NOT also poke EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR (0xE0080000) here:
+     QEMU virt doesn't ignore that write -- it raises a store-access-fault
+     that traps to m_trap_entry -> exception_handler, which reads Andes-only
+     mdcause (CSR 0x7C9) on default -cpu rv64 and re-traps forever.
+     If a fixture needs to fail-fast inside vsim, use rt_fail() in
+     runtime/rt_c_helpers.h, which writes SimCommand::FAILED to sim_control

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_minimal
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()
