content.text: Found the root cause: vsim's `handle_open` has an indexing bug — uses absolute guest VA `a0` as offset into `data_region.data`, missing the `- region.base_address` correction. This is harmless on Tier-1 (huge owned buffer) but segfaults on Tier-2 (narrow shared mmap). Let me fix it AND use a stack-independent epilogue so Tier-1 works for all fixtures (avoiding the vsim rebuild).

content.subject: Patch libgloss._exit to dual-path (sim_control + semihosting)
content.description: Edit /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c so _exit first writes EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR (vsim path) then issues the RISC-V semihosting SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED ebreak triple (QEMU virt path). Keep _write/HTIF paths as-is.

content.text: I'll fix both vsim HTIF bugs — base-address subtraction across all handlers AND newlib→Linux flag translation in `handle_open`/`handle_openat`. Then incremental rebuild.

The file /local/nick/vsim/src/htif/sys_call.hpp has been updated successfully.

lines: +// Convert a guest VA to a host-pointer into data_region.data. Subtracts
+// region.base_address so the index lands at the correct offset whether the
+// region starts at 0x0 (Tier-1, no shared mem) or at 0x80000000 (Tier-2,
+// --shared-mem-base, narrowed RAM). Without this, a guest stack VA like
+// 0x80700000 indexes 2 GiB into the data span, which is in-bounds for the
+// 0x90000000-byte owned buffer but OOB for a 128 MiB shared mmap.
+// Newlib's <sys/_default_fcntl.h> uses BSD-derived O_* values that differ
+// from the Linux ABI -- e.g. O_CREAT=0x200 in newlib vs 0x40 in Linux,
+// O_TRUNC=0x400 vs 0x200. Pass-through to host open() with newlib values
+// would clear O_CREAT/O_EXCL and silently accept O_APPEND, so a fopen("w+")
+// for a non-existent file fails with ENOENT. Translate flag-by-flag.

content.text: `sys_call.hpp` is `#include`d inside the `SimControl` class body — its functions are member functions. My helper needs to be a member, not free. Let me fix.

content.content: The file /local/nick/vsim/src/htif/sys_call.hpp has been updated successfully.

lines: +// NOTE: this header is #include'd inside the SimControl class body
+// (sim_control.hpp), so the functions here are member functions with implicit
+// `this`. The helpers below are member functions for the same reason -- they
+// reach `data_region`, which is a private SimControl member.
// Convert a guest VA to a host-pointer into data_region.data. Subtracts
// region.base_address so the index lands at the correct offset whether the
// region starts at 0x0 (Tier-1, no shared mem) or at 0x80000000 (Tier-2,
// 0x80700000 indexes 2 GiB into the data span, which is in-bounds for the
// 0x90000000-byte owned buffer but OOB for a 128 MiB shared mmap.
// O_TRUNC=0x400 vs 0x200. Pass-through to host open() with newlib values
// would clear O_CREAT/O_EXCL and silently accept O_APPEND, so a fopen("w+")
// for a non-existent file fails with ENOENT. Translate flag-by-flag.

content.text: **All four pass.** Let me capture the actual stdout to confirm output and tempfile lifecycle, then sanity-check the failure path.

input.command:
timeout 90 bash -c '
SHARED=$(mktemp -p /dev/shm vsim-rt-shared.XXXXXX)
truncate -s 134217728 "$SHARED"
QSTATE=$(mktemp); VSTATE=$(mktemp); LOG=$(mktemp)
trap "rm -f $SHARED $QSTATE $VSTATE $LOG" EXIT
for f in rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io; do
  echo "=== $f stdout ==="
  /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64 \
    -object "memory-backend-file,id=mem,share=on,mem-path=$SHARED,size=134217728" \
    -machine virt,memory-backend=mem -cpu andes-ax45mp \
    -bios none -nographic -kernel /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/$f.elf \
    -plugin /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/qemu_plugin/libhybrid_handoff.so,outfile=$QSTATE >/dev/null 2>&1
  timeout 60 /local/nick/vsim/build/sim_ax45mpv_premium /local/nick/vsim/hybrid/test/$f.elf \
    --resume-from-qemu $QSTATE --handoff-out $VSTATE \
    --shared-mem-path $SHARED --shared-mem-size 134217728 \
    --qemu-binary /local/nick/qemu_v5/build/qemu-system-riscv64 >$LOG 2>&1
  echo "RC=$?"
  grep -v "^\[" "$LOG" | grep -v "^Copyright\|^Version\|^$" | head -5
done
echo "=== /tmp/rt_c_file_io.tmp post-run (should be unlinked): ==="
ls -la /tmp/rt_c_file_io.tmp 2>&1 || echo "  (gone, as expected)"
'

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io rt_c_minimal
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

content.text:
## Summary

All 3 C-level fixtures pass end-to-end through the hybrid round-trip (QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2), and the 9 existing fixtures still pass.

**Results (CTest):**
- `test_e2e_rt_c_hello` — printf smoke; vsim log contains `hello from vsim`.
- `test_e2e_rt_c_printf` — format-specifier coverage; produces `dec=-42 hex=deadbeefcafe str=vsim chr=Z ptr=0x80000000 pad=[    7]`, etc.
- `test_e2e_rt_c_file_io` — `fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink` round-trip with self-validation; outputs `40 bytes round-tripped` and self-unlinks `/tmp/rt_c_file_io.tmp`. Failure path verified by inverting `memcmp` -> harness reports `RC=1`.

**Files added:**
- `hybrid/test/runtime/{crt0.S,trap.S,handler.c,htif.h,syscall.h,core_v5.h,platform.h,memory_map.h}` — verbatim from vsim-demo
- `hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c` — `_exit` rewritten to use RISC-V semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED` (the original `*0xE0080000=...` write segfaults under default `-cpu rv64` with unassigned-MMIO trap)
- `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` — linker script preserving `.data`/`.bss`/`.rodata` and exposing `_edata`/`_end`/`_stack=0x80700000`
- `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` — drain markers + `rt_fail()`
- `hybrid/test/rt_c_{hello,printf,file_io}.c`

**Files modified:**
- `hybrid/test/Makefile` — `RT_C_FIXTURES` list + rule (newlib linked, `-nostartfiles`, crt0 first in input so `_start` lands at `0x80000000`)
- `tests/hybrid/{roundtrip_e2e.sh,rt_shared_mem.sh}` — `-cpu andes-ax45mp` (gates Andes-only CSRs `mcache_ctl`/`mmisc_ctl` that crt0 writes); `rt_shared_mem.sh` accepts `FIXTURE`/`EXPECT_RC` env so C fixtures reuse the Tier-2 driver
- `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` — added `VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES` foreach
- `src/htif/sys_call.hpp` — fixed two pre-existing vsim bugs surfaced by HTIF file I/O on shared mem: every handler now uses `guest_va_to_host()` (subtracts `region.base_address`) instead of indexing `data + a0` directly, and `handle_open`/`handle_openat` translate newlib O_* (0x200/0x400) -> Linux O_* (0x40/0x200)

The C runtime is **Tier-2-only** (shared mmap). Tier-1 won't work because (a) main()'s ra-spill+restore around `csrwi 0x7C0,1` reads stack RAM that QEMU2 doesn't inherit, and (b) HTIF magicmem buffers sit at guest VA `~0x80700000` which OOB-faults vsim's narrowed shared region without the base-subtract fix.

input.command: git diff --cached --stat src/htif/sys_call.hpp 2>&1; echo "---"; git diff --cached src/htif/sys_call.hpp 2>&1 | head -40

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: fix HTIF guest-VA indexing and newlib->Linux flag translation

Two long-standing pre-existing bugs in sim_control's HTIF dispatch
surfaced when running newlib-linked C programs against vsim under
Tier-2 (`--shared-mem-path`).

1. Guest-VA indexing.  Most handlers indexed `data_region.data + a0`
   directly, treating a guest virtual address as if it were a buffer
   offset.  Worked by accident on Tier-1 (region [0x0..0x8FFFFFFF],
   2.25 GiB owned buffer absorbs even a stack VA like 0x80700000) but
   segfaulted on Tier-2, where the region is narrowed to
   [shared_mem_base..base+size-1] (typically 128 MiB).  Replaced with a
   `guest_va_to_host<T>(va)` helper that subtracts
   `region.base_address` first.  Applied to handle_open, _read,
   _write, _getcwd, _access, _faccessat, _fstat, _fstatat, _link,
   _lstat, _stat, _openat, _rename, _unlink, and gettimeofday.

2. open()/openat() flag translation.  The Andes newlib uses BSD-derived
   O_* values (O_CREAT=0x200, O_TRUNC=0x400, O_APPEND=0x8); host Linux
   uses O_CREAT=0x40, O_TRUNC=0x200, O_APPEND=0x400.  Passing the guest
   bitmap straight through to host open() silently dropped O_CREAT and
   confused O_TRUNC vs O_APPEND, so fopen("w+") of a missing file
   returned ENOENT.  Added a flag-by-flag translator covering the
   subset newlib stdio uses (RD/WR, APPEND, CREAT, TRUNC, EXCL,
   NONBLOCK, NOCTTY).

The error log line for handle_open/openat now prints both the guest
flag word and the translated Linux value, so future ABI drifts are
diagnosable from a failing run alone.

All 9 existing rt_*.S e2e fixtures still pass; new C-level fixtures
(rt_c_hello, rt_c_printf, rt_c_file_io, in a follow-up commit)
exercise both the indexing and the flag-translation paths.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
behavioral: add 3 C-level Tier-2 e2e fixtures linked against demo runtime

Stand up a C-level analogue of the Tier-1 rt_*.S fixtures, so e2e
tests can exercise newlib stdio, HTIF file I/O, and the demo runtime
end-to-end through QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2.

Fixtures (all in hybrid/test/, all expect_rc 0):

- rt_c_hello:    printf "hello from vsim" between drain markers,
                 confirms libgloss._write -> sim_control HTIF_PUTCHAR
                 byte-poke path.
- rt_c_printf:   format-specifier coverage (%d %lx %s %c %p %5d
                 %08x %-10s %o %u %ld) through newlib vfprintf.
- rt_c_file_io:  fopen/fwrite/fseek/fread/fclose/unlink round-trip
                 with in-program memcmp validation.  rt_fail() on
                 mismatch writes SimCommand::FAILED to sim_control,
                 vsim exits 1, harness reports the failure.

Runtime (hybrid/test/runtime/, copied from vsim-demo verbatim except
libgloss.c):

- crt0.S, trap.S, handler.c, htif.h, syscall.h, core_v5.h, platform.h,
  memory_map.h: brings up cache/FP/V/UNA bits, vectored mtvec, .bss
  clear, and the HTIF syscall dispatch table.
- libgloss.c: only _exit is rewritten to the RISC-V semihosting
  SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED ebreak triple.  The original demo's
  EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR write traps store-access-fault on QEMU virt's
  unassigned-MMIO and re-traps forever via mdcause, which is unmapped
  on default rv64 -- the ebreak triple is the only termination path
  QEMU 2 actually has.  vsim itself never reaches _exit (it terminates
  at the csrwi 0x7C0,1 drain marker), so dropping the MMIO write is
  safe; fail-fast inside vsim uses rt_fail() instead.
- rt_c.ld: replaces handoff_roundtrip.ld's /DISCARD/ catch-all with
  proper .rodata/.data/.bss sections and exposes _edata/_end (consumed
  by crt0's .bss memset) and _stack=0x80700000 (sits in QEMU virt's
  default 128 MiB RAM and inside vsim's RAM).
- rt_c_helpers.h: shared phase markers and rt_fail().

Why Tier-2 (shared mmap):

C frames spill ra to stack across printf/fopen calls; main()'s
return path after csrwi 0x7C0,1 reads ra back from sp+8.  Tier-1
gives QEMU 2 a fresh `-kernel` reload with virgin stack RAM, so ra
loads as zero and `c.jr ra` faults to mtvec.  Tier-2 mmaps the same
backing file across QEMU 1 / vsim / QEMU 2, so the stack survives
the boundary intact.

Wiring:

- hybrid/test/Makefile: gains $(RT_C_FIXTURES) list and a per-fixture
  rule that links the runtime, includes runtime/, and passes
  -nostartfiles + -T runtime/rt_c.ld.  crt0.S is listed first so its
  .text lands at the load address and _start ends up at 0x80000000
  regardless of input order.
- tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh: parameterized by FIXTURE/EXPECT_RC
  (defaults preserve the original rt_shared_mem behavior); QEMU 1
  spawn now passes `-cpu andes-ax45mp` so the cache/misc CSRs the
  demo crt0 writes don't trap illegal-instr.
- tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh: same `-cpu andes-ax45mp` change for
  Tier-1 driver so it stays consistent.
- cmake/HybridConfig.cmake: VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES foreach registers
  test_e2e_rt_shared_mem alongside the three new C fixtures.

Verified:

- ctest -R 'test_e2e_rt_c_': 3/3 pass.
- ctest -R 'test_e2e_rt_(pc_jump|all_gprs|sign_bits|abi_aliases|long_roi|mem_loadstore|unaligned_lh|csr_survival|shared_mem)$':
  9/9 pass (no regression from the harness `-cpu` change or the
  parameterization of rt_shared_mem.sh).
- Failure path verified by inverting the rt_c_file_io memcmp and
  rerunning -- harness reports `expected exit 0, got 1` and the FAIL
  message reaches vsim's stdout.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

message.content:
This session is being continued from a previous conversation that ran out of context. The summary below covers the earlier portion of the conversation.

Summary:
1. Primary Request and Intent:
   The user requested copying special vsim-demo runtime files (libgloss.c, handler.c, trap.S, crt0.S, plus headers) from `/local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo` into the vsim repo at `/local/nick/vsim`, linking them against e2e tests, and verifying both QEMU and vsim could execute them correctly via the hybrid simulator. After understanding the constraints, user requested: (1) explain why vsim needs those files and why QEMU can execute them, (2) design a C-level hello-world e2e test running via hybrid simulator, (3) write more C-level e2e tests to verify printf and file read/write, (4) commit the changes, (5) push them. The hybrid simulator flow is QEMU 1 -> vsim -> QEMU 2 round-trip with `csrwi 0x7C0, 0` and `csrwi 0x7C0, 1` drain markers.

2. Key Technical Concepts:
   - Hybrid simulator round-trip: QEMU 1 (drains state at csrwi 0x7C0,0) -> vsim (resumes, runs, drains at csrwi 0x7C0,1, exits 200) -> QEMU 2 (spawned by vsim with `-semihosting-config enable=on,target=native`, exits via RISC-V semihosting ebreak triple)
   - RISC-V semihosting `SYS_EXIT_EXTENDED` (reason 0x20026) via `slli x0,x0,0x1f; ebreak; srai x0,x0,0x7` triple
   - vsim's `sim_control` peripheral at MMIO 0xE0080000 (exit) and 0xE0080004 (HTIF_PUTCHAR) and 0xE0080010 (HTIF syscall packet)
   - Andes-specific CSRs (mcache_ctl 0x7CA, mmisc_ctl 0x7D0, mxstatus 0x7C4, mdcause 0x7C9, milmb 0x7C0) requiring `-cpu andes-ax45mp` instead of default rv64
   - Tier-1 (no shared mem, registers-only across handoff) vs Tier-2 (`--shared-mem-path` with mmap-backed memory-backend-file shared between QEMU 1, vsim, QEMU 2)
   - Newlib BSD-derived O_* flags (O_CREAT=0x200, O_TRUNC=0x400, O_APPEND=0x8) vs Linux O_* (O_CREAT=0x40, O_TRUNC=0x200, O_APPEND=0x400)
   - HTIF magicmem packet: 8 uint64s {n, a0..a6} written to 0xE0080010 by `frontend_syscall`
   - Stack-survival issue: main()'s ra-spill to sp+8 around csrwi marker; QEMU 2's `-kernel` ELF reload doesn't restore stack RAM in Tier-1
   - Linker script must preserve .data/.bss/.rodata (existing handoff_roundtrip.ld discards them)

3. Files and Code Sections:

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/crt0.S` (verbatim from vsim-demo)
     - Provides `_start` entry point at 0x80000000, brings up cache/FP/V/UNA bits, .bss clear via memset, calls init_mtvec then main then _exit
     - Critical: must be FIRST in linker input order so `_start` lands at the load address

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/trap.S` (verbatim)
     - `m_trap_entry` saves x1..x31 + mepc/mstatus/mxstatus, calls `handle_trap`

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/handler.c` (verbatim)
     - `init_mtvec` sets mtvec=&m_trap_entry; `handle_trap` dispatches ECALL to syscall table, otherwise calls exception_handler -> _exit(mcause)

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/libgloss.c` (modified)
     - Original `_exit` wrote to EXIT_COMMAND_ADDR (0xE0080000) — this traps store-access-fault on QEMU virt with default rv64 unassigned MMIO, causing infinite trap loop via mdcause read
     - Rewritten to use only RISC-V semihosting:
     ```c
     __attribute__((used, noreturn)) void _exit(int exit_status) {
       static volatile uint64_t semihost_block[2];
       semihost_block[0] = 0x20026;
       semihost_block[1] = (uint64_t)(uint32_t) exit_status;
       register long a0 __asm__("a0") = 0x20;
       register long a1 __asm__("a1") = (long)(uintptr_t) semihost_block;
       __asm__ __volatile__(
         ".option push\n.option norvc\n"
         "slli x0, x0, 0x1f\nebreak\nsrai x0, x0, 0x7\n"
         ".option pop\n"
         : : "r"(a0), "r"(a1) : "memory");
       __asm__ __volatile__("1: j 1b");
       __builtin_unreachable();
     }
     ```

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c.ld` (new)
     - Replaces handoff_roundtrip.ld's /DISCARD/ catch-all
     - Loads at 0x80000000, preserves .text/.rodata/.data/.bss, exposes _edata/_end/_stack=0x80700000

   - `hybrid/test/runtime/rt_c_helpers.h` (new)
     ```c
     #define SIM_CONTROL_REG (*(volatile uint32_t*) 0xE0080000)
     #define SIM_FAILED 0x01234569u
     static inline void rt_phase_qemu1_drain(void) { asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 0"); }
     static inline void rt_phase_vsim_drain(void)  { asm volatile("csrwi 0x7C0, 1"); }
     __attribute__((noreturn)) static inline void rt_fail(void) {
       fflush(stdout);
       SIM_CONTROL_REG = SIM_FAILED;
       for (;;) {}
     }
     ```

   - `hybrid/test/rt_c_hello.c`
     ```c
     int main(void) {
       rt_phase_qemu1_drain();
       setvbuf(stdout, NULL, _IONBF, 0);
       printf("hello from vsim\n");
       rt_phase_vsim_drain();
       return 0;
     }
     ```

   - `hybrid/test/rt_c_printf.c`
     - printf with %d %lx %s %c %p %5d %08x %-10s %o %u %ld coverage

   - `hybrid/test/rt_c_file_io.c`
     - fopen("/tmp/rt_c_file_io.tmp","w+") -> fwrite -> fseek -> fread -> memcmp -> fclose -> unlink, with rt_fail() on mismatch

   - `hybrid/test/Makefile` (modified)
     ```make
     RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io
     RUNTIME_DIR := runtime
     RUNTIME_SRCS := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                     $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
     RT_C_CFLAGS := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                    -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall
     # crt0.S FIRST so _start lands at load address:
     $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
         $(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@
     ```

   - `tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh` (modified) - added `-cpu andes-ax45mp` to QEMU 1

   - `tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh` (modified)
     - Parameterized: `FIXTURE="${FIXTURE:-rt_shared_mem}"` and `EXPECT_RC="${EXPECT_RC:-0}"`
     - Added `-cpu andes-ax45mp`

   - `cmake/HybridConfig.cmake` (modified)
     ```cmake
     set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
       "rt_shared_mem:0"
       "rt_c_hello:0"
       "rt_c_printf:0"
       "rt_c_file_io:0"
     )
     foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
       string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
       list(GET parts 0 fname)
       list(GET parts 1 erc)
       add_test(NAME test_e2e_${fname}
         COMMAND bash -c "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh")
       set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77 LABELS "hybrid-e2e")
     endforeach()
     ```

   - `src/htif/sys_call.hpp` (modified)
     - Added `guest_va_to_host<T>(va)` helper subtracting `data_region.region.base_address`
     - Added `newlib_to_linux_open_flags()` translator
     - Patched all 14 handlers (handle_open, _read, _write, _getcwd, _access, _faccessat, _fstat, _fstatat, _link, _lstat, _stat, _openat, _rename, _unlink, gettimeofday) to use the helper
     - Critical: helpers must be member functions (no `static`) because sys_call.hpp is `#include`d inside the SimControl class body

4. Errors and Fixes:
   - QEMU 1 hung on first instruction (csrsi mcache_ctl, 1): mcache_ctl is Andes-only CSR 0x7CA. Default rv64 traps illegal-instr, mtvec=0, infinite re-trap. Fixed by adding `-cpu andes-ax45mp` to roundtrip_e2e.sh and rt_shared_mem.sh.
   - QEMU 2 hung after vsim exit: dual-path _exit wrote to 0xE0080000 (unassigned MMIO on QEMU virt). The store-access-fault traps to mtvec -> exception_handler -> reads mdcause (Andes-only) -> traps -> infinite loop. Fixed by making _exit use only semihosting (vsim never reaches _exit anyway since it terminates at csrwi 0x7C0,1).
   - Stack-survival issue: main()'s `c.ldsp ra,8(sp)` after the marker reads from QEMU 2's virgin stack RAM. Fixed by switching to Tier-2 shared mmap.
   - rt_c_file_io segfault in vsim: handle_open used `data.begin() + a0` directly (a0 is guest VA 0x8000b5c0). Worked by accident on Tier-1 (huge owned buffer) but OOB on Tier-2 (128MB shared mmap). Fixed by adding `guest_va_to_host(va)` helper that subtracts region.base_address.
   - rt_c_file_io ENOENT: newlib O_CREAT=0x200 != Linux O_CREAT=0x40, so host open() received 0x602 = O_RDWR|O_APPEND|O_TRUNC without O_CREAT. Fixed by adding newlib_to_linux_open_flags() translator.
   - Build error "invalid use of member 'data_region' in static member function": helper was declared `static inline` but sys_call.hpp is included inside SimControl class. Fixed by removing `static`.
   - Build dir CMakeCache pointed to /work (Docker path): Fixed by clearing dep CMakeCache.txt files and resetting ExternalProject stamps for systemc-cci.
   - _start at 0x80000030 instead of 0x80000000: linker put main first because rt_c_hello.c was first in input order. Fixed by changing Makefile rule to pass $(RUNTIME_SRCS) before $<.
   - User feedback: "don't implement now" early on, then "I don't know. The purpose is for hybrid simulator to run." - guided me to focus on hybrid simulator compatibility.

5. Problem Solving:
   - Identified that demo runtime is fundamentally an Andes-simulator runtime; needs `-cpu andes-ax45mp` for QEMU and Tier-2 shared mmap for stack survival
   - Discovered and fixed two pre-existing vsim HTIF bugs: indexing and flag translation
   - Verified fail-fast path works by inverting memcmp condition in rt_c_file_io
   - Confirmed all 9 existing rt_*.S fixtures still pass after harness changes
   - Final results via CTest: 3/3 C-level fixtures pass (test_e2e_rt_c_hello 12.40s, test_e2e_rt_c_printf 23.31s, test_e2e_rt_c_file_io 17.88s)
   - vsim stdout shows: "hello from vsim", "dec=-42 hex=deadbeefcafe str=vsim chr=Z ptr=0x80000000 pad=[    7]", "rt_c_file_io: 40 bytes round-tripped"

6. All user messages:
   - "refer to /local/nick/vsim-workspace/vsim-demo. The vsim demo needs to link special files like VSIM_FILES := ../libgloss.c ../handler.c ../trap.S VSIM_OPTIMIZE := -nostartfiles ../crt0.S copy them to this repo and link them against the e2e tests. see if qemu and vsim could execute them correctly"
   - "I don't know. The purpose is for hybrid simulator to run."
   - "first, let me understand why vsim needs those files. second, explain why those files could be executed via qemu."
   - "Now, you understand the issue. design a c-level e2e test like hello world and run using the hybrid simulator"
   - "Tool loaded." (multiple times)
   - "review the plan again with max effort. write more c-level ene-to-end test cases to verify (read/write files, printf)"
   - "commit these changes"
   - "push it"

7. Pending Tasks:
   - Complete rebase onto remote (remote has 3 new commits including `62970b1 structural: relocate hybrid contract headers and TCG plugin to hybrid_sim` which may collide with my work)
   - Push the two commits (20054c0 HTIF fix, d5a6e71 C-level e2e suite) after rebase

8. Current Work:

Tried `git pull --rebase` which failed: "cannot pull with rebase: You have unstaged changes" due to pre-existing `external/systemc-cci` submodule churn and `test_plan.md` deletion (neither of which I introduced). 9. Optional Next Step:

# Build tiny RV64 fixtures used by hybrid smoke tests.
# Default toolchain matches /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/...

TOOLCHAIN ?= /local/nick/SW_Release/build-ast540/build-toolchain/linux/nds64le-elf-newlib-v5d
CC        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-gcc
LD        := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-ld
OBJDUMP   := $(TOOLCHAIN)/bin/riscv64-unknown-elf-objdump

CFLAGS  := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -nostdlib -static -Wall
LDFLAGS := -T spin.ld -nostdlib -static

# Tier-1 round-trip e2e fixtures share handoff_roundtrip.ld.
RT_FIXTURES := rt_all_gprs rt_abi_aliases rt_sign_bits rt_pc_jump \
               rt_long_roi rt_mem_loadstore rt_unaligned_lh \
               rt_shared_mem rt_csr_survival

# Tier-1 C-level fixtures share runtime/ (crt0+trap+libgloss+handler) and
# runtime/rt_c.ld. Newlib is linked (no -nostdlib) for printf/fopen/...
RT_C_FIXTURES := rt_c_hello rt_c_printf rt_c_file_io
RUNTIME_DIR   := runtime
RUNTIME_SRCS  := $(RUNTIME_DIR)/crt0.S $(RUNTIME_DIR)/trap.S \
                 $(RUNTIME_DIR)/libgloss.c $(RUNTIME_DIR)/handler.c
RT_C_CFLAGS   := -march=rv64gc -mabi=lp64d -O1 -static -nostartfiles \
                 -I$(RUNTIME_DIR) -Wall

.PHONY: all clean dump

all: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf handoff_roundtrip.elf \
     $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

spin.elf: spin.S spin.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c spin.S -o spin.o
	$(LD) $(LDFLAGS) spin.o -o $@

handoff.elf: handoff.S handoff.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff.S -o handoff.o
	$(LD) -T handoff.ld -nostdlib -static handoff.o -o $@

handoff_exit.elf: handoff_exit.S handoff_exit.ld
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_exit.S -o handoff_exit.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_exit.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_exit.o -o $@

handoff_roundtrip.elf: handoff_roundtrip.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c handoff_roundtrip.S -o handoff_roundtrip.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static handoff_roundtrip.o -o $@

$(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.S handoff_roundtrip.ld semihost_exit.inc
	$(CC) $(CFLAGS) -c $< -o $*.o
	$(LD) -T handoff_roundtrip.ld -nostdlib -static $*.o -o $@

# C-level fixtures: compile+link via gcc (so newlib is pulled in). The shared
# runtime is rebuilt per-fixture, which is fine -- they're tiny and isolating
# each ELF avoids stale-object pitfalls. crt0.S is passed FIRST so _start
# lands at the load address (0x80000000) -- ld preserves input order within
# the .text wildcard.
$(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf): %.elf: %.c $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c_helpers.h
	$(CC) $(RT_C_CFLAGS) -T $(RUNTIME_DIR)/rt_c.ld $(RUNTIME_SRCS) $< -o $@

dump: spin.elf handoff.elf handoff_exit.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d spin.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff.elf
	$(OBJDUMP) -d handoff_exit.elf

clean:
	rm -f spin.o spin.elf handoff.o handoff.elf handoff_exit.o handoff_exit.elf \
	      handoff_roundtrip.o handoff_roundtrip.elf \
	      $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.o) $(RT_FIXTURES:%=%.elf) \
	      $(RT_C_FIXTURES:%=%.elf)

# Hybrid simulator (QEMU + Verilator) build integration.

option(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID "Enable hybrid QEMU+Verilator co-simulation" OFF)

set(VSIM_QEMU_ROOT "/local/nick/qemu_v5"
  CACHE PATH "Path to QEMU source tree used to build the hybrid TCG plugin")

# The ABI test is always built so struct layout drift is caught even in
# non-hybrid builds. It has no SystemC/Verilator dependency.
vsim_add_test(test_state_abi tests/hybrid/state_abi.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_abi PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_abi)

# Shared-mem smoke: glues MmapBacking + SimpleMemory and verifies bytes are
# visible through an independent mmap of the same file.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_smoke tests/hybrid/shared_mem_smoke.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_smoke PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_smoke ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_smoke)

# Section 9: validates the narrowed QEMU-virt RAM region + mmap composition.
vsim_add_test(test_shared_mem_offset tests/hybrid/shared_mem_offset.test.cpp)
target_link_libraries(test_shared_mem_offset PRIVATE
  lib_fmt
  lib_spdlog
  lib_systemc
  ${VSIM_LINK_LIBS}
)
add_dependencies(test_shared_mem_offset ext_fmt ext_spdlog ext_systemc)
add_dependencies(check test_shared_mem_offset)

vsim_add_test(test_state_loader tests/hybrid/state_loader.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_loader PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_loader)

vsim_add_test(test_abstract_command tests/hybrid/abstract_command.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_abstract_command)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_driver tests/hybrid/resume_driver.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_driver PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_driver)

vsim_add_test(test_insn_match tests/hybrid/insn_match.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_insn_match PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_insn_match)

vsim_add_test(test_resume_from_qemu tests/hybrid/resume_from_qemu.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_resume_from_qemu PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_resume_from_qemu)

vsim_add_test(test_memory_map tests/hybrid/memory_map.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_memory_map)

vsim_add_test(test_csr_trigger tests/hybrid/csr_trigger.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_csr_trigger PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_csr_trigger)

vsim_add_test(test_state_drain tests/hybrid/state_drain.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_state_drain PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_state_drain)

vsim_add_test(test_gdbstub_client tests/hybrid/gdbstub_client.test.cpp)
add_dependencies(check test_gdbstub_client)

vsim_add_test(test_handoff_controller tests/hybrid/handoff_controller.test.cpp)
target_include_directories(test_handoff_controller PRIVATE
  "${CMAKE_SOURCE_DIR}/hybrid/include"
)
add_dependencies(check test_handoff_controller)

if(VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID)
  if(NOT EXISTS "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/qemu-plugin.h")
    message(WARNING
      "VSIM_ENABLE_HYBRID=ON but qemu-plugin.h not found under "
      "${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT}/include/qemu/. Build the TCG plugin out-of-tree via "
      "hybrid/qemu_plugin/Makefile on a host that has QEMU installed.")
  endif()
  message(STATUS "Hybrid mode: enabled (QEMU root: ${VSIM_QEMU_ROOT})")

  # Round-trip e2e tests. Each entry registers one CTest case using the
  # parameterized roundtrip_e2e.sh harness. The harness skips (return 77)
  # when QEMU/plugin/ELF artifacts are missing, so the same CTestTestfile
  # works whether ctest is invoked from the container's /work/build or
  # from the host's build/.
  set(VSIM_RT_FIXTURES
    "handoff_roundtrip:3"
    "rt_pc_jump:0"
    "rt_sign_bits:0"
    "rt_abi_aliases:0"
    "rt_long_roi:0"
    "rt_all_gprs:0"
    "rt_mem_loadstore:0"
    "rt_unaligned_lh:0"
    "rt_csr_survival:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/roundtrip_e2e.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Tier-2: separate driver because it allocates /dev/shm and uses
  # --shared-mem-path / -object memory-backend-file to share RAM across
  # QEMU 1, vsim, and QEMU 2.
  set(VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES
    "rt_shared_mem:0"
    # C-level fixtures: linked against vsim-demo C runtime (crt0/trap/libgloss/
    # handler). They need Tier-2 (shared mmap) so the stack and heap survive
    # the vsim->QEMU2 boundary -- main()'s ra-spill/restore around the
    # csrwi 0x7C0,1 marker would otherwise read uninitialized RAM in QEMU2.
    "rt_c_hello:0"
    "rt_c_printf:0"
    "rt_c_file_io:0"
  )
  foreach(entry ${VSIM_RT_T2_FIXTURES})
    string(REPLACE ":" ";" parts ${entry})
    list(GET parts 0 fname)
    list(GET parts 1 erc)
    add_test(
      NAME test_e2e_${fname}
      COMMAND bash -c
        "FIXTURE=${fname} EXPECT_RC=${erc} bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_shared_mem.sh"
    )
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_${fname} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()

  # Negative / robustness drivers: each guards a quietly-degrading failure
  # path the happy-path round-trips can't see (corrupted state file,
  # missing --qemu-binary, silent gdbstub peer).
  foreach(neg corrupt_state no_qemu_binary gdbstub_timeout)
    add_test(NAME test_e2e_rt_${neg}
      COMMAND bash ../tests/hybrid/rt_${neg}.sh)
    set_tests_properties(test_e2e_rt_${neg} PROPERTIES
      SKIP_RETURN_CODE 77
      LABELS "hybrid-e2e"
    )
  endforeach()
endif()
